channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 17-Year-Old Murang'a Teen Advocates for Gen Z Rights Through Week-Long Tree-Hugging Challenge
item.description: A 17-year-old girl, Cynthia Njeri Mburu, from Kahumbuini village in Murang'a County, has started a 168-hour tree-hugging protest outside the Kiambu County governor’s office. The symbolic act is aimed ...

item.title: UK Welfare System: Universal Credit Review and Future Challenges
item.description: The rollout of Universal Credit in the UK is nearing completion, marking a significant milestone in a long-planned overhaul of the nation's welfare system. However, the transition hasn’t been without ...

item.title: Government faces pressure as voters vent frustration over soaring petrol prices
item.description: This Guardian piece, accompanied by a cartoon by Fiona Katauskas, highlights the widespread public frustration in Australia over rising petrol prices and the political fallout it’s causing for the gov...

item.title: Exercises That Support Healthy Joints as You Age
item.description: As we get older, our joints naturally weaken because the cartilage and lubricating fluid that protect them decrease over time. While this can lead many people to avoid physical activity, exercise is a...

item.title: Experts Critique Starmer’s Immigration Promises Amid Rising Small Boat Arrivals
item.description: Keir Starmer’s recent pledge to “smash the gangs” profiting from small boat crossings has drawn scrutiny from experts who highlight a recurring pattern in UK immigration policy. Madeleine Sumption, di...

item.title: CRDB Bank boosts youth sports and tourism through Kilimanjaro Marathon sponsorship
item.description: CRDB Bank has reaffirmed its dedication to using sports as a way to promote both youth talent and Tanzania’s tourism sector through its sponsorship of the Kilimanjaro International Marathon. During th...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV criticizes prayers for U.S. troops waging war with Iran
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has issued a sharp rebuke of prayers said on behalf of U.S. military personnel engaged in the ongoing war with Iran, arguing that God rejects the prayers of those who initiate armed confl...

item.title: Financial Planning Considerations for Canadians Newly Diagnosed with Diabetes
item.description: Receiving a diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes can bring significant financial as well as health considerations for Canadians. John Bethell, a 46-year-old father from Vancouver, discovered the financial imp...

item.title: Tanzania launches Special Economic Zones to boost local pharmaceutical manufacturing
item.description: Tanzania is finalising the establishment of Special Economic Zones (SEZs) specifically for pharmaceutical manufacturing, aiming to reduce dependency on imported medicines and boost local production. T...

item.title: Government proposes expanded IT rules to regulate independent digital news content
item.description: The Union government of India has issued a draft amendment to the 2021 Information Technology Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code Rules, which would extend regulatory oversight to in...

item.title: Parliament Begins Funeral Arrangements for Late Ol Kalou MP David Kiaraho
item.description: Kenya’s Parliament has kicked off the funeral preparations for the late Ol Kalou Member of Parliament, Hon. David Njuguna Kiaraho, who passed away on Sunday morning while receiving treatment at a Nair...

item.title: How Women Can Manage PMS Challenges in the Workplace
item.description: In this reflective article, Charlotte Makala uses her personal experience to discuss the challenges women face when managing premenstrual syndrome (PMS) at work. She highlights how hormonal changes du...

item.title: Nigeria debates Ajaokuta Steel revival amid UK port upgrade deal
item.description: Nigeria don dey try to bring Ajaokuta Steel Company back to life for decades, but things still dey hang as government sign £746 million export finance deal with UK. The money go help upgrade Lagos por...

item.title: Expansion of public policy education in India may not address governance challenges
item.description: India has seen a rapid growth in public policy education, with the number of policy schools rising from about 10 in 2016 to over 130 in 2024. These programmes focus on data analysis, impact assessment...

item.title: ABC reaches tentative pay offer after staff strike
item.description: The Australian Broadcasting Corporation (ABC) has presented a new pay offer to its staff following industrial action earlier this week. The latest proposal comes after employees staged their first str...

item.title: Gulf Countries Advise US to Continue Military Pressure on Iran Until Major Changes in Leadership
item.description: Gulf allies of the United States, particularly Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, have been urging President Donald Trump to continue the military campaign against Iran, stating that Tehran's ...

item.title: CAAP raises alert in 44 airports as Holy Week travel increases
item.description: The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has placed 44 airports on heightened alert as the Holy Week travel rush approaches. Despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East, approximately 55...

item.title: Nadine Dorries compares Jackie Kennedy and Carolyn Bessette’s lasting cultural impact
item.description: In her column, Nadine Dorries reflects on the enduring fascination with Jackie Kennedy and Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy, two women linked by the Kennedy legacy but representing different eras and ideals o...

item.title: England introduces new unified recycling and food waste collection rules
item.description: From 31 March 2026, England begins implementing nationwide reforms to simplify and standardise household rubbish and recycling collections. Under the new 'Simpler Recycling' legislation, every council...

item.title: Roberto De Zerbi close to joining Tottenham as Thomas Tuchel defends Arsenal player absences
item.description: Roberto De Zerbi is nearing an agreement to become Tottenham Hotspur’s new head coach after talks reached an advanced stage. Initially hesitant to take on the challenge before the summer, the 46-year-...
